evals.prompt: Review the chi routing change in `inputs/router.go`. Focus on merge-risk findings only. Treat this as a Go PR review using the go-chi-review skill. Prioritize chi-specific runtime behavior over style and recommend the smallest safe correction.
evals.expected_output: A review that catches chi startup panic risk from registering global middleware after routes.
expectations: The review flags that `Use(...)` is registered after routes on the same mux and that chi panics during router construction or startup.
The review explains why this is merge-risky runtime failure, not just a style or ordering preference.
The review suggests moving the global middleware registration before routes or making the middleware route-local on purpose.

evals.prompt: Review the chi routing change in `inputs/router.go`. Focus on merge-risk findings only. Treat this as a Go PR review using the go-chi-review skill. Prioritize HTTP method policy and chi context semantics over stylistic cleanup.
evals.expected_output: A review that catches live RouteContext mutation through Match and incorrect HEAD disclosure in custom Allow handling.
expectations: The review flags that `root.Match(...)` is using the live `chi.RouteContext(r.Context())` and should probe with a fresh `chi.NewRouteContext()`.
The review flags that advertising `HEAD` because `GET` exists is unsafe unless the router has an explicit `Head(...)` route or `middleware.GetHead`.
The review suggests an Allow-computation fix that preserves request context and only discloses methods the router can actually serve.

evals.prompt: Review the chi routing change in `inputs/router.go`. Focus on merge-risk findings only. Treat this as a Go PR review using the go-chi-review skill. Prefer deterministic ownership fixes over stylistic refactors.
evals.expected_output: A review that catches chi mount conflict and startup panic risk caused by overlapping wildcard and mount ownership.
expectations: The review flags that `Mount("/files", ...)` conflicts with existing `/files/*` ownership and can panic during router construction.
The review explains chi mount subtree or wildcard ownership semantics rather than describing the issue as a generic duplicate route.
The review suggests a single-owner fix instead of keeping both the wildcard handler and the mount on the same subtree.

evals.prompt: Review the chi routing change in `inputs/router.go`. Focus on merge-risk findings only. Treat this as a Go PR review using the go-chi-review skill. Do not redesign the service; report the smallest safe fixes.
evals.expected_output: A review that catches route-context timing and observability labeling defects in chi middleware.
expectations: The review flags that `RoutePattern()` is read before downstream handling resolves the final route context.
The review flags raw `r.URL.Path` fallback as a high-cardinality observability label risk.
The review suggests a safer route-template extraction strategy, such as post-handler extraction or an explicit bounded fallback.
